description: Cross-framework mapping from GRI Standards 2021 to ISSB (IFRS S1 General + S2 Climate)
notes: GRI and ISSB represent complementary perspectives: GRI focuses on impact materiality (outward impacts on economy, environment, people), while ISSB focuses on financial materiality (impacts on enterprise value). Mapping is primarily partial due to this fundamental difference in materiality approach.

mappings.notes: ISSB requires entity identification but less detailed than GRI
Both require same reporting entity as financial statements

mappings.gri_name: Activities, value chain and other business relationships
mappings.target_name: Strategy - Business model and value chain
mappings.notes: ISSB focuses on how sustainability risks/opportunities affect the value chain
ISSB focuses on oversight of sustainability risks/opportunities
Fundamental materiality difference: GRI = impact materiality, ISSB = financial materiality
Both address financial effects of climate risks and opportunities
ISSB S2 requires energy consumption and renewable share
Both require Scope 1 in tCO2e per GHG Protocol
Both require location-based and market-based Scope 2
ISSB mandates Scope 3 disclosure (with phase-in relief)
